growth_records.cite: Nikel et al. Reconfiguration of metabolic fluxes in Pseudomonas putida as a response to sub-lethal oxidative stress. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00884-9. [PMC8163872]
growth_records.snippet: the corresponding molar yields of gluconate and 2-ketogluconate on glucose in control experiments were yG/S = 0.31 ± 0.09 and yK/S = 0.12 ± 0.02 C-mol C-mol−1, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Control (no H2O2) glucose batch culture of P. putida KT2440. Text explicitly states secretion product molar yields on glucose: gluconate yG/S = 0.31 ± 0.09 C-mol C-mol^-1; 2-ketogluconate yK/S = 0.12 ± 0.02 C-mol C-mol^-1 (periplasmic sugar-oxidation products, later re-consumed). μ, qS and Yx/s were determined (regression of log-transformed OD600 during exponential growth; Yx/s at 24 h after glucose exhaustion) but their numeric values are only in Fig 1c image, not in the text, so left null (not invented). Culture temperature not stated in Methods (only extraction/drying temps given), so temperature_C null. Reported catabolic NADPH overproduction (intracellular, not a secretion) R_NADPH surplus = 1.3 mmol gCDW^-1 h^-1 under control — noted for context only.

growth_records.cite: Nikel et al. Reconfiguration of metabolic fluxes in Pseudomonas putida as a response to sub-lethal oxidative stress. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00884-9. [PMC8163872]
growth_records.snippet: In the presence of oxidative stress, yG/S and yK/S were 0.19 ± 0.08 and 0.05 ± 0.01 C-mol C-mol−1 ... the flux of NADPH generation increased to 14.4 ± 0.3 mmol gCDW−1 h−1 upon exposure of the cells to H2O2
growth_records.notes: Sub-lethally H2O2-stressed (1.5 mM) glucose batch culture. Secretion product molar yields (halved vs control, reflecting reduced periplasmic glucose oxidation): gluconate yG/S = 0.19 ± 0.08; 2-ketogluconate yK/S = 0.05 ± 0.01 C-mol C-mol^-1. μ, qS and Yx/s were reported not significantly different from control but numeric values are only in Fig 1c image (left null, not invented). Intracellular NADPH generation flux increased to 14.4 ± 0.3 mmol gCDW^-1 h^-1 (~50% surplus over biosynthetic demand) under stress — intracellular flux, not a secretion, noted for context. Culture temperature not stated in Methods.

growth_records.cite: Yang et al. Comparing in planta accumulation with microbial routes to set targets for a cost-competitive bioeconomy. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2122309119. [PMC9335188]
growth_records.snippet: Muconic acid is produced from glucose using engineered P. putida at 35 °C for 89 h. The final yield was 0.378 mol/mol glucose (or 0.298 g/g glucose) ... with a production rate of 0.1 g/L/h, corresponding to 50% of the theoretical yield.
growth_records.notes: Secondary data cited within a techno-economic analysis. Reported: muconic acid yield 0.378 mol/mol glucose = 0.298 g/g glucose (50% of theoretical max 0.739 mol/mol = 0.583 g/g); volumetric production rate 0.1 g/L/h; 35 °C, 89 h. Volumetric rate is reported directly (not computed). No biomass reported so specific mmol/gDW/h not derivable. No growth rate reported. Note: elsewhere the paper cites E. coli reaching 36.8 g/L in a 2-L fed-batch reactor and C. glutamicum reaching 85 g/L (different hosts, not the modeled P. putida process; not curated as separate records because their yields/rates are not individually stated).

growth_records.snippet: the highest volumetric productivity for 2-FMA (q2-FMA) = 83 +/- 2 umol l-1 h-1 ... During the first 3 h, the dilution rate (D) was D = 0.9 h-1 ... The dilution rate was then reduced to D = 0.4 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Continuous biofilm biotransformation. Two dilution phases: D=0.9 h^-1 for 3 h (wash out planktonic cells; OD600 fell from ~3 to ~0.3) then D=0.4 h^-1 for production (recorded). Reported volumetric productivity q_2-FMA = 83 +/- 2 umol/L/h (2-FMA secretion). Because product-forming biomass is a retained biofilm, D does NOT equal specific growth rate mu, so no mu recorded; biomass in gDW not reported so specific (mmol/gDW/h) rates not derivable. 3-FBz feed 10 mM accumulated to ~8 mM, 2-FMA peaked ~0.5 mM. Bioreactor temperature not restated in text (P. putida cultivated at 30 C elsewhere in the study); left null to avoid inventing. Batch-phase specific growth rates (mu) shown only graphically (Fig 3F) with no absolute numeric value in text (switch strain within 12% of control).
